summary: Indexed (palette-based) PNG images are corrupted and produce non-deterministic output after decode+encode using SKBitmap.Decode()/Encode() — a regression since 2.80.0 caused by a bug in Skia's PNG indexed-color codec.

bugSignals.errorMessage: Result PNG image is broken/corrupted; image pixels differ on each run

stepsToReproduce: Observe that destImage.png has corrupted/wrong colors
Run multiple times to observe non-deterministic output

repoLinks.description: Upstream Skia fix for indexed PNG codec issue identified by community member
PR to cherry-pick the upstream fix into mono/skia (SkiaSharp's Skia fork)

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Issue was reported with 2.88.3; the upstream Skia fix was identified but it is unknown whether the fix was merged into the mono/skia fork used by SkiaSharp 3.x.

regression.reason: Community member @themcoo confirmed regression started with 2.80.0 and previous versions handled indexed images correctly. The upstream Skia fix (commit 983ae8625b) resolves the issue.

analysis.summary: The PNG codec in native Skia (libSkiaSharp) mishandles indexed/palette-based PNG images during decode, resulting in corrupt pixel data and non-deterministic output on re-encode. This is a regression introduced in the Skia version bundled with SkiaSharp 2.80.0. Upstream Skia fixed this in commit 983ae8625b05737e7528fac074030d11e225961d; a PR (mono/skia#98) was created to bring the fix into SkiaSharp's Skia fork, but its merge status is unknown.
analysis.rationale: Clear wrong-output bug with complete minimal repro and regression confirmation. Root cause was independently identified by a community contributor tracing the issue to upstream Skia's indexed PNG color handling. The bug is in the native C++ layer (Skia's PNG codec), making area/libSkiaSharp.native the correct area. The behavior is non-deterministic (image changes per run), pointing to uninitialized memory or palette-index confusion in the codec. A workaround exists by converting the bitmap color type before encoding.

keySignals.text: The result image changes when you try my sample code for several times
keySignals.interpretation: Non-deterministic output strongly suggests uninitialized memory or stale palette data in the native PNG codec path.

keySignals.text: This started with 2.80.0. Previous versions were handling this correctly.
keySignals.interpretation: Confirmed regression — root cause is in a change made to the native Skia build between 2.x versions.

keySignals.text: I found the issue and fix in google/skia repo: https://github.com/google/skia/commit/983ae8625b05737e7528fac074030d11e225961d
keySignals.interpretation: Root cause precisely identified in upstream Skia. Cherry-picking the fix confirmed it resolves the issue.

keySignals.text: When I cherry-picked this change and rebuilt the libskiasharp.dll your image is decoded/encoded fine.
keySignals.interpretation: Fix confirmed to work. Resolution path is to incorporate the upstream commit into mono/skia.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKBitmap.Decode(SKCodec) reads codec.Info, then calls codec.GetPixels(bitmapInfo, bitmap.GetPixels()). For indexed PNGs, the codec.Info will reflect the indexed color type; any bug in the native SkPngCodec's handling of the palette maps directly to corrupt pixels returned here.

SKBitmap.Copy(SKColorType colorType) exists and calls CopyTo() which re-renders pixel data via SKPixmap — converting from indexed to Rgba8888 bypasses the buggy codec path entirely and provides a valid workaround.

SKBitmap.Encode(Stream dst, SKEncodedImageFormat format, int quality) is the encode path used in the repro. No issue in the C# wrapper layer itself; corruption originates from the previously decoded native pixels.

workarounds: Convert the decoded bitmap to Rgba8888 color type before encoding using bitmap.Copy(SKColorType.Rgba8888). This bypasses the indexed-color codec path that contains the bug.
nextQuestions: Was mono/skia PR #98 merged? If so, which SkiaSharp version includes the fix?
Does the issue reproduce with SkiaSharp 3.x (e.g., 3.119.3)? The upstream Skia milestone update may have incorporated the fix.
Is the corruption cross-platform (Linux, macOS) or only Windows?

resolution.hypothesis: Skia's PNG codec (SkPngCodec) incorrectly handles the palette/index lookup for indexed PNG images during decode, writing corrupt or uninitialized palette data into the pixel buffer. The upstream fix (commit 983ae8625b) corrects this.

proposals.title: Workaround: convert color type before encoding
proposals.description: Convert the decoded bitmap to Rgba8888 before encoding to avoid the indexed color codec path.

proposals.title: Fix: merge upstream Skia commit 983ae8625b into mono/skia
proposals.description: Cherry-pick or include google/skia commit 983ae8625b05737e7528fac074030d11e225961d in the mono/skia fork to fix the native PNG codec. A PR (mono/skia#98) was already created for this.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Workaround: convert color type before encoding
resolution.recommendedReason: Immediately actionable for affected users. The native fix requires the maintainer to verify and merge mono/skia#98 (or confirm it was included in a later Skia milestone update).

actionability.reason: Root cause is confirmed in the native Skia PNG codec. A community-provided upstream fix exists (commit 983ae8625b, PR mono/skia#98). Needs investigation to confirm whether the fix was incorporated in SkiaSharp 3.x. If already fixed, should close-as-fixed; otherwise the native fix needs to be merged.

actions.description: Apply type/bug, area/libSkiaSharp.native, os/Windows-Classic, backend/Raster, tenet/reliability labels
Post analysis with confirmed workaround and status of upstream fix
